title: Electrical Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can auto-route PCB layouts, generate schematic-to-netlist checks, and draft compliance documentation, but power-system safety margins and novel circuit topology design stay with the engineer.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted EDA tools (auto-placement, DRC checking, signal-integrity analysis) are mature in mainstream PCB and chip-design suites.
rationales.adoption: Semiconductor and consumer-electronics firms use AI-assisted layout and verification broadly; industrial and utility electrical engineering adopts more slowly.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run design-rule checks, regression test suites, and compliance-doc generation end to end, but field commissioning and safety-critical sign-off require a licensed engineer.

tasksAtRisk: Automated PCB placement, routing, and design-rule checking
Drafting standard compliance and test documentation
Running routine signal-integrity and power-analysis simulations
tasksGrowing: Novel circuit topology and power-system architecture design
Field commissioning and safety-critical system validation
Cross-domain integration with mechanical and software teams
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2071, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Layout, routing, and compliance paperwork are increasingly automated by AI-assisted EDA tools. Electrical engineers who specialize in power-system architecture, safety-critical validation, and system integration hold the ground AI can't reach yet.
